verses.text: At the temple a man lame from birth asks alms at the Beautiful gate. Daily need. He begs for coins, not knowing a greater gift is near.
At the temple a man lame from birth asks alms at the Beautiful gate. Daily need. He begs for coins, not knowing a greater gift is near.
At the temple a man lame from birth asks alms at the Beautiful gate. Daily need. He begs for coins, not knowing a greater gift is near.
'Silver and gold have I none... In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth rise up and walk.' Better than gold. The apostle gives what money cannot buy.
'Silver and gold have I none... In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth rise up and walk.' Better than gold. The apostle gives what money cannot buy.
'Silver and gold have I none... In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth rise up and walk.' Better than gold. The apostle gives what money cannot buy.
He takes him by the hand; his feet receive strength; he walks, leaping and praising God. Instant cure. The healed man cannot contain his joy.
He takes him by the hand; his feet receive strength; he walks, leaping and praising God. Instant cure. The healed man cannot contain his joy.
The people see him and are filled with wonder, running to Solomon's porch. Drawing wonder. The miracle gathers a crowd to hear.
The people see him and are filled with wonder, running to Solomon's porch. Drawing wonder. The miracle gathers a crowd to hear.
The people see him and are filled with wonder, running to Solomon's porch. Drawing wonder. The miracle gathers a crowd to hear.
Why marvel? not our power, but 'his name through faith' made him whole. Christ glorified. Peter turns all the glory to Jesus.
Why marvel? not our power, but 'his name through faith' made him whole. Christ glorified. Peter turns all the glory to Jesus.

Ye did it in ignorance; 'repent... that your sins may be blotted out.' A call to turn. God offers pardon even to His Son's murderers.
Ye did it in ignorance; 'repent... that your sins may be blotted out.' A call to turn. God offers pardon even to His Son's murderers.
Ye did it in ignorance; 'repent... that your sins may be blotted out.' A call to turn. God offers pardon even to His Son's murderers.
God will send Jesus; the prophet like Moses must be heard. Heed Him. To refuse Christ is to be cut off from the people.
God will send Jesus; the prophet like Moses must be heard. Heed Him. To refuse Christ is to be cut off from the people.
God will send Jesus; the prophet like Moses must be heard. Heed Him. To refuse Christ is to be cut off from the people.
God will send Jesus; the prophet like Moses must be heard. Heed Him. To refuse Christ is to be cut off from the people.
All the prophets foretold these days; God sent His Son first to bless you, 'turning away every one... from his iniquities.' Blessing in turning. Christ's first gift is freedom from sin.
